recommendation:
Peter,

Everyone assumes AI hits a power wall. The real bottleneck is bad scheduling: we waste the power we already have.

In simulated replay of 1.5M public production requests, my system delivers +724% more SLA-safe goodput per dollar than the production scheduler.

You fund definite, contrarian theses about hidden constraints. This one is hiding in plain sight.

I'm Beckett, 17, building Aurelius. It forecasts power constraints and ranks scheduling decisions by economic outcome before executing.

I can send you the one-page technical memo.
label: best-supported candidate under the current assumptions; no reliable distinction between finalists (uncalibrated evaluator — real outreach outcomes, not more simulation, would sharpen this)
